text: I find myself in a dynamic, thoughtfully designed interior—its angles and colors subtly unsettle my sense of orientation. The first scene feels open but somewhat impersonal; there is space, distance, a faint hum of anticipation, yet a sense of waiting lingers. Encountering the second scene, I am drawn into an exhibit about research and development, surrounded by vibrant visuals and words. Here, I feel a little more grounded by the focus on human curiosity and technological coexistence. I sense both the stimulation of new ideas and the weight of existential questions about our place among machines. Intrigue and reflection keep me attentive; I navigate a landscape of innovation, but also quiet uncertainty.
